af_score_components.notes: MCP server for Lunar MCPX API gateway (quality 68). API key auth. Gateway/proxy for aggregating multiple MCP servers with centralized governance, rate limiting, retries, and cost tracking. Production requires commercial licensing despite MIT source. README is light on implementation details — must consult docs.lunar.dev. Circuit breakers and retry logic are core features. Good for enterprise multi-MCP orchestration workflows needing centralized control. MCPX zero-code aggregator promising but maturity unclear.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS expected for cloud deployment. API key auth — scope granularity not well documented. Open core model — security posture depends on deployment mode (self-hosted vs cloud). Startup maintainer — dependency hygiene less rigorous than established vendors. API keys via configuration. Acts as gateway proxy — security of downstream MCP servers compounds risk. Commercial licensing required for production security guarantees.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted or commercial — free tier may not have SLA. Unknown version stability — relatively new product. Unclear breaking change policy for open core components. Retry guidance documented — retry and circuit breakers are core product features. Adding gateway layer introduces additional failure modes. Production reliability requires commercial tier. Promising for resilience patterns but maturity of MCPX aggregator is unclear.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Adobe Commerce (Magento) enterprise e-commerce REST and GraphQL API. Multiple auth methods: admin tokens, customer tokens, integration tokens (OAuth). Integration tokens have configurable resource access scopes — best for agents. GraphQL and REST cover different feature sets — some operations only available in one interface. Bulk import API for large catalog changes (async, file-based). EAV data model makes bulk operations slow. Instance-specific API base URL requires prior discovery. Good for enterprise e-commerce automation but steep complexity.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced on modern Magento. OAuth2 integration tokens with configurable resource access scopes — per-resource scoping available. Admin tokens expire (configurable). Adobe maintained for Commerce edition. PCI-DSS/GDPR compliance. Self-hosted deployment puts patch management on customer — frequent security patches required for PHP ecosystem. Tokens via secure config. Scope configuration for integration tokens enables least-privilege. Admin API has high blast radius — create dedicated integration with minimal scopes for agents.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — no SLA unless on managed cloud hosting (Adobe Commerce Cloud). Stable versioned API within major versions. Migration guides exist but Magento major version upgrades are complex. No retry guidance documented. Partial idempotency — some operations safe to retry, others not. EAV data model performance degrades without proper Elasticsearch/OpenSearch. API performance highly dependent on infrastructure and caching (Varnish, Redis). Async bulk import API for large-scale catalog operations.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Magic.link passwordless authentication Admin REST API. Secret key for server-side admin operations — simple for agents. DID tokens (Magic's auth tokens) expire after 15 minutes — agents must validate freshness before use. Public key vs secret key distinction requires careful handling. User metadata storage is limited to what Magic stores. Multi-language SDK (Python, Node, Ruby, Go, PHP). Good for auth automation agents that need to manage users, validate tokens, or manage sessions. SOC2/GDPR certified.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced — this is an auth platform. Secret key for admin API, public key for client-side — strong separation of concerns. No scope granularity beyond public/secret key distinction. SOC2/GDPR compliant. Being an auth platform means Magic's own security posture is critical. Secret keys via environment variables — never expose in browser. DID token validation requires server-side verification. Magic.link email domain considerations for corporate email systems.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS auth platform with expected SLA and status page. Stable API versioning. Good migration guides. No retry guidance explicitly documented. DID token expiry (15 min) creates complexity for long-running agents — must implement token refresh. Cursor-based pagination for user listing. Admin API rate limits not well documented. Auth availability is business-critical — design for graceful degradation if Magic is unavailable.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Mailchimp email marketing API. API key (full account access) or OAuth2 (no scopes). List members addressed by MD5 hash of lowercased email — agents must hash emails before API calls. Audience IDs required for almost every operation and differ per account. Mailchimp silently deduplicates subscribers on add — updates rather than errors. Unsubscribing via API keeps contact as 'unsubscribed'; deletion is separate. Good for marketing automation agents. Offset pagination. SDK available for Python, PHP, Ruby, Node, Java.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key provides full account access — no scope restriction per key. OAuth2 available but also without scopes. Mailchimp (Intuit) maintained. GDPR/CAN-SPAM compliance for email marketing. API key via environment variable. Full-access API keys mean agents can access all lists, campaigns, audiences. No read-only API key option. Audience data contains subscriber PII — appropriate data governance required. Treat API key as high-privilege credential.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Major SaaS platform with SLA and status page. Stable versioned API. Good migration guides. No retry guidance explicitly documented. Offset pagination for list operations. Email sending not idempotent — retrying sends duplicates. Subscriber upsert (via MD5 hash) is idempotent. Rate limits documented but can be aggressive for bulk list management. GDPR data export/deletion operations have processing delays — not instant.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Mailgun transactional email API — developer-focused with strong routing, inbound parsing, and validation features. API key + domain-based authentication (HTTP Basic Auth). Domain verification required before sending. Free tier expires after 3 months — unexpected billing risk for agents in production. EU data residency requires explicitly selecting EU region. Inbound email routing requires MX record changes. Cursor-based pagination. Multi-language SDK. Good for transactional email in agent workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. SOC2/GDPR compliance. API key with domain context — domain-level separation provides some isolation. No per-operation scope granularity. Sinch-owned (Mailgun) maintained. API key via environment variable. Webhook signature verification required for inbound email security. Domain verification prevents unauthorized sending. EU data residency option for GDPR. Credit card required — billing events possible for agent-triggered high volume.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with SLA and status page. SOC2. Stable API versioning. Good migration guides. No retry guidance documented. Email sending has no idempotency — retrying sends duplicate emails. Cursor-based pagination for log retrieval. Domain verification is a prerequisite — failure blocks all sending. Free tier 3-month expiry creates unexpected billing surprises for agents. Inbound routing MX changes have DNS propagation delays.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Make (formerly Integromat) workflow automation management API. API key Bearer token, simple for agents. Scenarios execute asynchronously — webhook trigger returns immediately, results must be fetched via execution logs. Operation counting differs from task/zap counting — loops multiply operations significantly. Free tier 1000 operations/month consumed quickly by looping scenarios. Scenario blueprints (JSON export/import) enable programmatic scenario deployment but format is undocumented. Webhook custom responses require specific scenario design. Offset pagination.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. SOC2/GDPR/ISO27001 compliance. API token provides access to all scenarios in account — no scope restriction. Bearer token via environment variable. Team/organization tokens available for shared access. No read-only token option documented. Scenario execution can trigger any connected app — significant blast radius. Webhook secret verification recommended for inbound webhooks. EU-based company (Czech) with GDPR compliance.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with SLA and status page. SOC2. Stable versioned API. Good migration guides. No retry guidance documented. Async scenario execution creates complex error recovery — must poll execution logs for results. No idempotency for scenario triggers. Operation limits (monthly quota) can block agents unexpectedly. Webhook scenario responses are not guaranteed real-time. Blueprint format for programmatic deployment is undocumented — fragile for infrastructure-as-code approaches.

af_score_components.notes: Local MCP server for context-aware structural code search (quality 75). No auth required — runs entirely locally. No rate limits. Semantic file discovery by intent rather than exact string match. Go-to-definition, find-references, impact analysis, session-based context carryover. MANTIC_MAX_FILES default 5000 may need tuning for large repos. Slower than ripgrep for raw text speed. Semantic reranking may require transformer model download. Good for AI agents needing intelligent file discovery in large codebases and monorepos with 100K+ files.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local tool — no network exposure. No credentials required or stored. Zero data egress by design. Read-only structural analysis operations. Community maintained — dependency hygiene depends on maintainer diligence. No compliance certifications (local tool). Local filesystem access bounded to project directory. Safe for codebases with sensitive data — no external transmission. macOS/Linux local access controls provide implicit security boundary.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted local tool — no SLA. Unknown version, newer project. No formal breaking change policy. Idempotency: yes — structural queries are deterministic. Large APKs/repos consume significant memory — may OOM on very large codebases. Plugin/transformer model download required for semantic reranking. MANTIC_MAX_FILES tuning required for large repos. Not suitable for production agent pipelines needing guaranteed uptime. Good for developer-local agent workflows.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Mapbox geocoding, directions, navigation, and geospatial API. Access tokens (pk.* public, sk.* secret) with URL restrictions — simple for agents. Geocoding v5 vs v6 response format differs — use v6 (Mapbox Search) for latest. Rate limits per-token — multiple agents sharing same token share limits. Country-specific data quality varies. Retry guidance documented. Full idempotency on all GET requests. Excellent docs. Batch geocoding available with size limits. Good for geo/logistics automation agents.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. SOC2/GDPR compliance. Public (pk.*) and secret (sk.*) token types with URL restriction configuration — tokens can be scoped to specific domains/URLs. Secret tokens via environment variable. Token scopes must be carefully configured — wrong scope gives confusing 401 errors. Mapbox (Amazon-backed) maintained. URL restrictions prevent token abuse if leaked. No per-operation scope granularity beyond public/secret split. Rate limits per-token — protect tokens to prevent quota exhaustion.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with SLA and status page. SOC2. Stable versioned API with good backward compatibility. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Full idempotency on all geocoding and routing queries — GET-based. Rate limits per-token require planning for multi-agent setups. Geocoding v5→v6 migration is a known breaking change — must update if using newer features. Batch geocoding has per-request size limits. Excellent overall reliability posture for geospatial automation.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Adobe Marketo Engage B2B marketing automation REST API. CRITICAL: Marketo returns HTTP 200 even for partial failures — always check 'success' field and 'errors' array in response body. Instance-specific base URL (Munchkin Account ID) must be fetched first. Daily API call limits (50k) exhausted quickly by bulk operations. Bulk APIs are async with file-based export — very different pattern from standard REST. Lead deduplication logic is complex. OAuth2 client credentials only (no API key option). Access token must be refreshed every 3600 seconds.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR/HIPAA compliance — enterprise-grade. OAuth2 client credentials flow — stronger than simple API key. No scopes — access is all-or-nothing per API user role. Adobe maintained. Client credentials via environment variables. High compliance posture for regulated industries. Lead and contact data is sensitive marketing PII — data governance critical. No per-resource scope control. Full-access API user means agents can access all leads, campaigns, and programs.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Enterprise SaaS with SLA. Stable API for core operations. Migration guides exist but enterprise upgrade paths are complex. Retry guidance documented but implementation is tricky — HTTP 200 on errors requires response body inspection before retrying. Daily API call limits (50k) create hard availability boundaries for bulk workflows. Async bulk APIs require polling export job status. Token refresh every 3600s must be embedded in agent wrapper. Cursor pagination for large result sets.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server (mcp_server_quality: 0). Open-source self-hosted data catalog (Go + Svelte). No authentication documented in README — authentication details unclear for self-hosted deployment. Very limited documentation. No error handling information. No rate limits (self-hosted). Requires PostgreSQL backend. Documentation primarily on external site, not in README. No SDK. Would need custom REST API integration or CLI for agent access. Early-stage project with minimal adoption signals. Not ready for agent integration without significant custom work.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends entirely on deployment configuration — not enforced by default. No authentication documented — open endpoint by default for self-hosted deployments. No scope control. Community maintained Go/Svelte project — dependency hygiene depends on maintainer. No compliance certifications. PostgreSQL credentials are deployment responsibility. Security score of 0 in existing evaluation reflects fundamental security gaps. Not suitable for production agent workflows without custom hardening.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted with no SLA. Unknown version — early-stage project. No formal breaking change policy. Unknown idempotency behavior. No error recovery documentation. Requires PostgreSQL backend management. Single binary deployment simplifies ops but no HA configuration documented. Not suitable for production agent workflows. Data catalog queries likely idempotent (read-heavy). Early-stage adoption means significant rough edges expected.
